title: Surfaces, Draw Order, Alpha, Transforms, Assets, and Presentation · Behavior Case: Surface Model
summary: Every frame reloads assets, repeatedly rotates transformed images, mixes alpha modes, draws UI behind the world, converts before display setup, and calls flip from several objects. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the surface model increment in owned layered rendering and asset pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory…
objectives: Distinguish display and off-screen surfaces, pixel formats, per-surface and per-pixel alpha, color keys, locks, and conversion timing.
Handle resize, minimize, restore, high-DPI, fullscreen, and zero extents without corrupting world state.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: asteroids-surface-model — Distinguish display and off-screen surfaces, pixel formats, per-surface and per-pixel alpha, color keys, locks, and conversion timing.
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-render-surfaces-assets-5
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-render-surfaces-assets-5.
# Case 9036: a controller-remapping accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save.
def worked_asteroids_render_surfaces_assets_5(logical_draws: int = 14, presented_frames: int = 14, alpha_assets: int = 4, converted_assets: int = 4, transform_from_original: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "playerworked5",
        "seed9036",
        "input5dekrow",
        "frameworked59036",
        "state9036worked5",
        "playtest5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if logical_draws < 1 or alpha_assets < 0:
        return False, logical_draws, "render-shape"
    if presented_frames != logical_draws:
        return False, presented_frames, "presentation-drift"
    if converted_assets != alpha_assets:
        return False, converted_assets, "surface-format"
    if not transform_from_original:
        return False, alpha_assets, "destructive-transform"
    assert presented_frames == logical_draws
    return True, converted_assets, "owned-render-pipeline"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Surface Model evidence · Theory Surface Model
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-surface-model-10vmyi" that uses case 2411 to compose draw order, surfaces, alpha, asset conversion, transforms, and one explicit frame presentation. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Surface Model evidence · Theory Surface Model.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Surface Model into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Surface Model evidence · Theory Surface Model, keep Surface Model connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-surface-model-10vmyi
# Competency: asteroids-surface-model.
# Case 2411: a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling.
# Operating constraint: retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity.
# Changed case: cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity.
def evidence_asteroids_surface_model_10vmyi(logical_draws: int = 14, presented_frames: int = 14, alpha_assets: int = 4, converted_assets: int = 4, transform_from_original: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_10vmyi = "10vmyi-iymv01-2411"
    assert evidence_variant_10vmyi
    evidence_axes_10vmyi = (
        "player10vmyi",
        "seed2411",
        "inputiymv01",
        "frame10vmyi2411",
        "state241110vmyi",
        "playtestiymv012411",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_10vmyi)) == 6
    if logical_draws < 1 or alpha_assets < 0:
        return False, logical_draws, "render-shape"
    if presented_frames != logical_draws:
        return False, presented_frames, "presentation-drift"
    if converted_assets != alpha_assets:
        return False, converted_assets, "surface-format"
    if not transform_from_original:
        return False, alpha_assets, "destructive-transform"
    assert presented_frames == logical_draws
    return True, converted_assets, "owned-render-pipeline"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Surface Model model · Theory Surface Model
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Surface Model: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 1458 during a controller-remapping accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to compose draw order, surfaces, alpha, asset conversion, transforms, and one explicit frame presentation; retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Distinguish display and off-screen surfaces, pixel formats, per-surface and per-pixel alpha, color keys, locks, and conversion timing.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Surface Model model · Theory Surface Model.
steps.why: Retrieving Surface Model through the concrete owned layered rendering and asset pipeline case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Distinguish display and off-screen surfaces, pixel formats, per-surface and per-pixel alpha, color keys, locks, and conversion timing.
